issues.title: Missing customer_id in GROUP BY
issues.problem: The query is missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause, which will cause an aggregation error.

issues.title: Case sensitivity in table names
issues.problem: Table names should be consistently cased to avoid confusion and potential errors.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which can be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT customer_id, merchant_id, SUM(amount)
FROM fact_transactions
JOIN dim_merchant ON fact_transactions.merchant_id = dim_merchant.merchant_id
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY customer_id, merchant_id;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the GROUP BY clause and some readability issues. The corrected query includes the missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause and adheres to consistent casing.
